channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: sports
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: sports

item.title: AEW stars Adam Copeland, Christian Cage talk Pure Plank, how planking revitalized their lives
item.description: All Elite Wrestling stars Adam Copeland and Christian Cage, whose real name is Jay Reso, launched and developed Pure Plank. They talked to Fox News Digital about the equipment.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Fox News Digital Sports NFL power rankings after Week 13 of 2024 season
item.description: The Thanksgiving holiday was make or break for a lot of teams in the NFL. Some teams separated themselves with respect to playoff contention.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Wiggins, Schieffelin and Clemson's defense hold down No. 4 Kentucky for a 70-66 win
item.description: CLEMSON, S.C. (AP) — Chauncey Wiggins scored 12 points and Ian Schieffelin had 11 points and a career-high 20 rebounds as Clemson shut down No. 4 Kentucky for a 70-66 victory Tuesday night in the SEC/ACC Challenge. The Tigers (8-1) held the Wildcats (7-1) to 30 points fewer than their average of 96.
item.dc:creator: PETE IACOBELLI, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Cougars go cold in 83-64 loss at Providence
item.description: PROVIDENCE, R.I. (ABC4 Sports) - The BYU basketball team suffered its worst shooting performance of the season and lost at Providence Tuesday night, 83-64 to fall to 6-2 on the season. The Cougars shot just 33.3 percent from the field in scoring a season-low 64 points.
item.dc:creator: Dana Greene, ABC 4

item.title: Knicks beat Magic to advance in the NBA Cup. Orlando also moves on as the East wild card
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Karl-Anthony Towns had 23 points and 15 rebounds, Jalen Brunson scored 21 points and the New York Knicks beat the Orlando Magic 121-106 on Tuesday to reach the quarterfinals of the NBA Cup. New York went 4-0 in East Group A and will host Atlanta on Dec. 11.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: St. John's to honor late coach Lou Carnesecca at men's and women's games this weekend
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — The men's and women's basketball teams at St. John's will wear “Lou” patches on their uniform jerseys for the rest of this season to honor Hall of Fame coach Lou Carnesecca, who died Saturday at age 99. A funeral mass at St.
item.dc:creator: MIKE FITZPATRICK, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Robbie Gould resigns as head football coach at Rolling Meadows High School
item.description: District 214 officials have announced the resignation of former Chicago Bears kicker and Rolling Meadows Mustangs head football coach, Gould, after a 5-5 season.
item.dc:creator: Eli Ong, WGN 9

item.title: Transgender powerlifter asks Minnesota Supreme Court to let her compete in women's events
item.description: ST. PAUL, Minn. (AP) — A transgender athlete should be allowed to compete in the women's division at powerlifting events because she's protected against discrimination by the Minnesota Human Rights Act, her attorneys urged the state Supreme Court on Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: STEVE KARNOWSKI, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trans female weightlifter takes USA Powerlifting lawsuit to Minnesota Supreme Court
item.description: A transgender powerlifter has taken a yearslong case of discrimination to the Minnesota Supreme Court, with opening arguments beginning on Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Miami's playoff hopes nosedive as Alabama rises in the latest College Football Playoff rankings
item.description: Miami’s playoff hopes took a nosedive while Alabama’s got a boost in the last rankings before the College Football Playoff bracket is set next weekend.
item.dc:creator: Eddie Pells, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: BYU runners use olive oil to win NCAA championship
item.description: The school's coach said his runners used “an old-school running trick” by rubbing olive oil on their legs to keep themselves warm.
item.dc:creator: Liz Jassin, NewsNation

item.title: McCaffrey injury potentially rules him out for rest of season
item.description: ORCHARD PARK, N.Y. (AP) — San Francisco running back Christian McCaffrey suffered a knee injury that coach Kyle Shanahan described as “potentially” season-ending in the 49ers’ 35-10 loss to the Buffalo Bills on Sunday night.
item.dc:creator: Alex Baker, KRON4
